messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0019",
  "title": "16 NEOCRAFT 从流量博弈转向“效率革命”与“方法论迁移”",
  "page_index": 17,
  "page_range": "17"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0020",
    "title": "赛道升维： IAP精品方法论对 IAA赛道的向下兼容",
    "page_range": "17",
    "summary": "This section addresses mobile game monetization trends and strategic publishing methodologies. It can answer retrieval queries regarding:\n- **Topics & Entities:** IAP (In-App Purchase) vs. IAA (In-App Advertising) monetization, hybrid monetization models, Europe/US mobile game market, NEOCRAFT, MMO and card game genres.\n- **Definitions:** IAP = 内购 (In-App Purchase); IAA = 广告变现 (In-App Advertising).\n- **Claims:** IAP download volumes are declining in Europe/US; IAA and hybrid models demonstrate strong market resilience; the strategic opportunity lies in applying mature IAP publishing frameworks to IAA games to elevate the LTV ceiling of light games.\n- **Metrics:** IAA and hybrid products account for ~30% of total mobile game revenue; LTV (Lifetime Value) is cited as a key performance metric to be optimized.\n- **Procedures/Methods:** Systematically transferring heavy-game IAP operational practices (fine-grained numerical modeling, long-term operations) to the IAA track.\n- **Dates, Tables & Figures:** No specific dates, tables, or figures are provided.\n- **Evidence Types:** Market trend observations and strategic industry analysis from NEOCRAFT."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0021",
    "title": "品类蓝海：突破“舒适区”的渗透机会",
    "page_range": "17",
    "summary": "This section addresses retrieval queries regarding:\n- **Topics:** Overseas game market expansion, identifying category \"blue oceans,\" and strategies for Chinese developers to break out of established competitive comfort zones.\n- **Entities:** Chinese game publishers/developers, overseas markets, specific game genres (puzzle/match-3, sorting, racing, action, shooting).\n- **Metrics:** Current market penetration rate of Chinese developers in high-traffic overseas genres (<10%).\n- **Claims:** Chinese firms hold established advantages in puzzle and sorting categories overseas but remain underrepresented in high-traffic genres; these low-penetration areas represent significant incremental growth potential.\n- **Procedures/Strategies:** How to execute targeted market entry by integrating localized publishing concepts with high-quality, genre-specific customization.\n- **Evidence Types:** Market penetration statistics and strategic industry analysis.\n- **Dates/Tables/Figures/Definitions:** None explicitly provided in this section."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0022",
    "title": "数据基石：误差控制在 10%以内的确定性发行",
    "page_range": "17",
    "summary": "**Topics:** Risk control in user acquisition, predictive modeling, deterministic publishing strategies.\n**Entities/Markets:** Mature markets (Europe, US); proprietary “流水半衰期” (Revenue Half-Life) monitoring model; LTV (Lifetime Value) model.\n**Metrics & Thresholds:** LTV prediction error ≤10%; supported payback cycles of 3–9 months.\n**Procedures/Methods:** Utilizes a self-developed “Revenue Half-Life” monitoring model to forecast LTV, providing decision support for varying payback periods and stabilizing marketing spend allocation.\n**Claims:** High-precision predictive models are the core of risk control in mature markets. Keeping LTV model error within 10% guarantees the stability and safety of user acquisition investments across 3–9 month return windows.\n**Evidence Types:** Internal proprietary model performance metrics; comparative market practice assertions. No tables or figures are included in this section."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0023",
    "title": "技术驱动： AI赋能的全链路增效",
    "page_range": "17",
    "summary": "**Topics:** AI-driven efficiency in game publishing, full-link AI integration, marketing and operations optimization, dual-drive strategy (data + technology).\n**Entities:** NEOCRAFT, AIGC, AdEagleEye (鹰眼监控系统), ROI.\n**Claims & Definitions:** AI serves as the foundational infrastructure for publishing efficiency rather than a supplementary tool. The \"ad-as-content\" model establishes a closed loop where marketing performance data directly informs game development (e.g., character creation optimization). NEOCRAFT's core strategy relies on parallel \"data-driven\" and \"technology-driven\" approaches.\n**Procedures & Methods:** \n- *Marketing:* Industrializes creative asset production via AIGC; extracts high-conversion hooks from viral ads and feeds them back into game design iterations.\n- *Operations:* Deploys monitoring systems (e.g., AdEagleEye) to combine short-term real-time tracking with long-term revenue forecasting; applies AI algorithms to optimize ad spend and maximize ROI.\n**Metrics:** ROI (Return on Investment) optimization.\n**Evidence & Content Types:** Strategic framework statements, operational workflow descriptions, proprietary system/tool references. No empirical data, case studies, or external citations are provided.\n**Dates, Tables & Figures:** None present in this section."
  }
]

message.content:
This section outlines NEOCRAFT’s strategic pivot from traditional traffic competition to an efficiency-driven publishing model, structured around methodology migration, category expansion, data precision, and AI integration. 

**Topics & Entities:** Mobile game monetization trends (IAP vs. IAA, hybrid models), overseas market expansion, category "blue oceans," risk-controlled user acquisition, and AI-driven publishing efficiency. Key entities include NEOCRAFT, Chinese game developers/publishers, the Europe/US mobile game market, and specific genres (MMO, card, puzzle/match-3, sorting, racing, action, shooting). Proprietary systems referenced include the “流水半衰期” (Revenue Half-Life) monitoring model and AdEagleEye (鹰眼监控系统).

**Definitions:** IAP = 内购 (In-App Purchase); IAA = 广告变现 (In-App Advertising). AI is positioned as foundational publishing infrastructure rather than a supplementary tool, operating under a parallel "data-driven + technology-driven" strategy.

**Claims:** IAP download volumes are declining in Europe/US, while IAA and hybrid models demonstrate strong market resilience. Applying mature IAP operational frameworks to IAA games can elevate the LTV ceiling of light games. Chinese developers hold established advantages in puzzle/sorting categories but remain underrepresented in high-traffic overseas genres, representing significant incremental growth potential. High-precision predictive modeling is the core of risk control in mature markets, and an "ad-as-content" model creates a closed loop where marketing performance data directly informs game development iterations.

**Metrics:** IAA and hybrid products account for ~30% of total mobile game revenue. LTV (Lifetime Value) and ROI (Return on Investment) are primary optimization targets. The proprietary LTV prediction model maintains an error threshold of ≤10%, supporting stable payback cycles of 3–9 months.

**Procedures/Methods:** Systematically transferring heavy-game IAP practices (fine-grained numerical modeling, long-term operations) to the IAA track. Executing targeted market entry by integrating localized publishing concepts with genre-specific customization. Utilizing the Revenue Half-Life model to forecast LTV, guide payback period decisions, and stabilize marketing spend allocation. Industrializing creative asset production via AIGC, extracting high-conversion ad hooks for game design iteration, and deploying AdEagleEye for real-time tracking combined with long-term revenue forecasting to optimize ad spend and maximize ROI.

**Evidence Types:** Market trend observations, strategic industry analysis, market penetration statistics, internal proprietary model performance metrics, strategic framework statements, and operational workflow descriptions.

**Dates, Tables & Figures:** No specific dates, tables, or figures are provided in this section.

response_text:
This section outlines NEOCRAFT’s strategic pivot from traditional traffic competition to an efficiency-driven publishing model, structured around methodology migration, category expansion, data precision, and AI integration. 

**Topics & Entities:** Mobile game monetization trends (IAP vs. IAA, hybrid models), overseas market expansion, category "blue oceans," risk-controlled user acquisition, and AI-driven publishing efficiency. Key entities include NEOCRAFT, Chinese game developers/publishers, the Europe/US mobile game market, and specific genres (MMO, card, puzzle/match-3, sorting, racing, action, shooting). Proprietary systems referenced include the “流水半衰期” (Revenue Half-Life) monitoring model and AdEagleEye (鹰眼监控系统).

**Definitions:** IAP = 内购 (In-App Purchase); IAA = 广告变现 (In-App Advertising). AI is positioned as foundational publishing infrastructure rather than a supplementary tool, operating under a parallel "data-driven + technology-driven" strategy.

**Claims:** IAP download volumes are declining in Europe/US, while IAA and hybrid models demonstrate strong market resilience. Applying mature IAP operational frameworks to IAA games can elevate the LTV ceiling of light games. Chinese developers hold established advantages in puzzle/sorting categories but remain underrepresented in high-traffic overseas genres, representing significant incremental growth potential. High-precision predictive modeling is the core of risk control in mature markets, and an "ad-as-content" model creates a closed loop where marketing performance data directly informs game development iterations.

**Metrics:** IAA and hybrid products account for ~30% of total mobile game revenue. LTV (Lifetime Value) and ROI (Return on Investment) are primary optimization targets. The proprietary LTV prediction model maintains an error threshold of ≤10%, supporting stable payback cycles of 3–9 months.

**Procedures/Methods:** Systematically transferring heavy-game IAP practices (fine-grained numerical modeling, long-term operations) to the IAA track. Executing targeted market entry by integrating localized publishing concepts with genre-specific customization. Utilizing the Revenue Half-Life model to forecast LTV, guide payback period decisions, and stabilize marketing spend allocation. Industrializing creative asset production via AIGC, extracting high-conversion ad hooks for game design iteration, and deploying AdEagleEye for real-time tracking combined with long-term revenue forecasting to optimize ad spend and maximize ROI.

**Evidence Types:** Market trend observations, strategic industry analysis, market penetration statistics, internal proprietary model performance metrics, strategic framework statements, and operational workflow descriptions.

**Dates, Tables & Figures:** No specific dates, tables, or figures are provided in this section.
